name: Bulenox
description_short: Bulenox is a futures prop firm offering funded accounts from $10K to $250K with 100% profit on first $10K earned and a 90/10 split beyond that.
description_long:
## The 100% First-$10K Payout Rule Is the Core Trade-Off

Bulenox is a futures prop firm built around a single unusual promise: you keep 100% of the first $10,000 you earn in the Master (funded) phase, then move to a 90/10 split beyond that. Every other decision about Bulenox — the platform, the pricing, the drawdown math, the instruments — flows from whether that structure fits how you actually trade. If you scalp micros and pull small, frequent payouts, the first-$10K rule can be worth thousands versus a standard 80/20 or 90/10 firm. If you swing large contracts and expect a single $40K month, most of your earnings still fall under the 90/10 split, and Bulenox becomes an ordinary prop firm with an above-average promotional cost structure.

This is a decision-focused review of Bulenox for futures traders comparing prop firms in 2026. It covers the account sizing, the platform constraint, the algo policy, the weaknesses of a younger firm, and where competitors like Apex, Topstep and Tradeify may actually be the better call.

## Account Sizes, Contract Limits and Drawdown Math

Bulenox sells simulated qualification accounts from roughly $25,000 up to $250,000, with two option paths at checkout: a No Scaling Account and an EOD (end-of-day drawdown) Account. Each tier has a matched contract cap, profit target and trailing drawdown that determine how aggressively you can size in:

- **$25K account** — max 3 contracts, $1,500 profit target, $1,500 drawdown, listed at $145/month.
- **$50K account** — max 7 contracts, $3,000 profit target, $2,500 drawdown, listed at $175/month with a "$50OFF" coupon dropping it to $125.
- **$100K account** — max 12 contracts, $6,000 profit target, $3,000 drawdown, listed at $215/month with a "$60OFF" coupon dropping it to $155.
- **$150K account** — max 15 contracts, $9,000 profit target, $4,500 drawdown, $325/month.
- **$250K account** — max 25 contracts, $15,000 profit target, $5,500 drawdown, $535/month.

The pattern is worth reading twice. A $250K account carries only $5,500 of drawdown against a $15,000 target — a 2.7x reward-to-risk in your favor, but with 25 contracts of exposure that can chew through that buffer in a single MES cluster. The $50K tier, by contrast, gives you $2,500 of drawdown against a $3,000 target — a much thinner cushion but at a lower monthly fee. Bulenox does not disable size, but the drawdown ratios push you toward disciplined micro sizing regardless of the label on the account.

## Why NinjaTrader 8 and Rithmic Shape Your Setup

Bulenox runs on NinjaTrader 8, with a Rithmic 14-day free trial available for new accounts. That combination matters. NinjaTrader is a genuinely strong platform for automated futures strategies, custom indicators and DOM-based scalping, but it is Windows-first, has a steeper learning curve than web-based competitors, and locks out anyone who has built their workflow around Tradovate, TradingView, TSTrader or Quantower. If you already use NinjaTrader with your own strategy scripts, Bulenox is the shortest path to a funded account you will find. If you have never opened NinjaTrader, expect a week of setup before your first real trade.

Rithmic data is preferred by most serious futures traders for its execution speed, and the free trial is a real cost saver — after the trial, market data is a recurring line item that many prop firm reviews ignore. Budget for it before signing up, especially at the higher account tiers where you may want CQG or Rithmic-tier data quality.

## Algos and EAs Are Fully Permitted — No Asterisks

One of the strongest reasons to consider Bulenox is its stance on automated trading. Expert Advisors, semi-automated setups and full algo systems are permitted on both qualification and Master accounts, with no per-day limit on automated fills and no ban on news trading of the kind Topstep and some FTMO-lineage firms enforce. For a NinjaTrader user with a working NinjaScript strategy, this is the closest thing to running your bot on a real account without posting collateral.

Where this gets sharper: Bulenox permits multiple accounts simultaneously. A trader running three uncorrelated strategies can put each on its own $50K or $100K account and combine the payouts. That workflow is not friendly to every strategy, but it removes a real friction point that firms with strict single-account rules impose.

## The Weaknesses Bulenox Won't Advertise

Three honest limitations deserve attention before signing up.

First, **Bulenox is futures only**. There are 42 tradable instruments including the harder-to-find Micro Bitcoin (MBT), but no stocks, no forex spot, no options and no CFDs. If your edge is in equity index futures or micros, this is fine. If you were hoping to run a mixed book with any non-futures instrument, you cannot. Bulenox is a specialist product, not a universal broker replacement.

Second, **Bulenox was founded in 2022 and operates under Bulenox LLC, headquartered in Wilmington, Delaware**. That is a shorter track record than Topstep (founded 2012), Apex Trader Funding (2021) or Earn2Trade. Prop firms have had a rough two years — several once-respected names have restructured payouts, changed rules mid-account, or paused withdrawals. A younger firm is not automatically a bad firm, but it is a firm with less public payout history to verify. Read recent trader forums for payout timelines before committing capital.

Third, **the monthly fee model recurs until you clear the qualification phase**. Bulenox pairs this with "no time limit" evaluations, which sounds generous but has a cost tail: a trader who takes six months to hit the profit target on a $100K account has paid over $900 in fees before earning a dollar. Frequent "$50OFF" and "$60OFF" coupons meaningfully reduce this, and stacking evaluations during 50-75% off promotions is the norm rather than the exception. Do not pay list price for a Bulenox account without checking their public discount calendar.

## Bulenox Versus Apex, Topstep and Tradeify in 2026

Bulenox competes most directly with Apex Trader Funding, Topstep, Earn2Trade and Tradeify. The honest split runs like this:

- **Choose Apex** if you want a broader platform choice (Tradovate, NinjaTrader, Rithmic-direct) and are comfortable with an 80/20 split above the initial threshold. Apex has a longer visible payout history and more third-party review coverage.
- **Choose Topstep** if you value educational structure, coaching and a well-known brand for its resale value on your trading resume. Topstep is stricter on rules but has been paying traders for over a decade.
- **Choose Tradeify** if you want a newer TradingView-first prop model with cleaner UX.
- **Choose Bulenox** if you specifically want the 100% first-$10K structure, you are a NinjaTrader-native trader, and you run algos that benefit from multiple concurrent accounts.

## The Real Buyer for a Bulenox Account

A Bulenox account makes sense for a specific trader: a futures day trader or micro-scalper who already runs NinjaTrader 8, has a strategy that produces frequent small payouts rather than one large monthly withdrawal, and can enter during a 50-75% off promotion to keep the qualification cost under $100. Algorithmic traders with tested EAs get additional value from the multi-account rule.

It makes less sense for a discretionary swing trader planning to hit a $40K month, a beginner without a proven futures edge, a non-futures trader, or anyone unwilling to learn NinjaTrader. For those profiles, the recurring fees will outweigh the 100% first-$10K advantage, and a more established competitor offers safer stakes on the capital of a firm that has already survived a rule-change cycle.
